reasoning: The question asks if Wesley So's FIDE classical ranking on the resolution date (early Feb 2026) will be 'as high or higher' (meaning numerically less than or equal to) his ranking on 2025-11-09. Since FIDE updates rankings monthly, there will be exactly three rating lists (Dec, Jan, Feb) between November and February. The probability includes cases where his ranking stays exactly the same, which typically happens around 20-25% of the time for top-10 players over a 3-month span if they do not play many classical games or if they match expectations. The remaining probability is split between improving (numerically lower rank) and declining (numerically higher rank). Due to the steady rise of young prodigies (e.g., Gukesh, Erigaisi, Abdusattorov, Firouzja) the chances of older top-10 players losing spots is slightly higher than gaining. Assuming a 20% chance of remaining strictly equal, a 35% chance of improving, and a 45% chance of dropping, the total probability of remaining 'as high or higher' evaluates to roughly 55%.
